scope.summary: Makes oracle_describe return a structured not-found or not-visible error for an empty catalog result and preserves the exact case of double-quoted owner and table identifiers.
out_of_scope: The specialized oracle_describe_index, oracle_describe_trigger, and oracle_describe_view tool contracts.
Live Oracle catalog verification; this change is covered by offline dispatcher and bound-argument tests.

entry_points.name: oracle_describe MCP tool dispatch
entry_points.trace: oracle_describe_not_visible_returns_structured_not_found_with_next_actions and oracle_describe_unresolved_synonym_returns_structured_not_found dispatch the tool through its production branch and assert OBJECT_NOT_FOUND, oracle_schema_inspect guidance, and no constraints query after a catalog miss.

entry_points.name: dictionary metadata lookup binding
entry_points.trace: oracle_describe_preserves_double_quoted_identifier_case proves the dispatcher sends AppOwner and camelCase unchanged to ALL_TAB_COLUMNS, while describe_constraints_binds_owner_and_table pins the exact bound column/constraint lookup contract.

limitations: Scoped offline tests only: cargo test -p oraclemcp oracle_describe -- --nocapture passed 3 tests. The full workspace gate was not run for this source commit.

An empty ALL_TAB_COLUMNS result cannot distinguish a nonexistent object from an object hidden by catalog visibility, so the envelope intentionally reports not found or not visible and directs the caller to visible-object discovery.
